title: Reasons for determination — July 3, 2026
summary: Canadian manufacturers of wooden bedroom furniture can now export to the U.S. without facing anti-dumping duties, as the CITT ended its investigation finding no evidence of unfair pricing practices.

title: Determination — June 18, 2026
summary: Canada's international trade tribunal ruled against imposing new import duties on steel products, allowing Canadian manufacturers to continue buying affordable foreign steel for production.

title: Determination — June 9, 2026
summary: Canadian importers of frozen French fries face new anti-dumping duties after the Canadian International Trade Tribunal found U.S. producers were selling below cost.

title: Reasons for determination — June 1, 2026
summary: The Canadian International Trade Tribunal ruled that imported steel pipe does not harm Canadian producers, so tariffs won't be imposed and Canadian buyers can import it freely.

title: Public hearing — May 25, 2026
summary: Canadian importers of certain goods may face higher tariffs if the Canadian International Trade Tribunal rules in favor of domestic producers claiming unfair import competition at the May 25 hearing.

title: Reasons for finding — May 19, 2026
summary: Canadian manufacturers of stainless steel fasteners now face a five-year tariff on imports from India after the CITT found dumping harmed domestic producers.

title: Determination — May 15, 2026
summary: Canada's International Trade Tribunal ruled that imported steel pipes don't harm domestic producers, so tariffs won't increase and Canadian buyers can access cheaper foreign pipes.
